creator: stephen@FreeBSD.org
text:
I wrote the last message before I read you other message.

This package is put together in a rather weird manner, not conforming to other octave packages.  I don't know enough about the octave packaging system to know what should be done.

I guess someone should check if it works on Linux.

To check whether it works, just write the name of one of its functions.  If it gives an error message "not found" or something like that, you know it isn't installed properly.  If you get a more meaningful error like "not enough arguments" you know it did install properly.
